root_text.an6.14:1.1: Tatra kho āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an6.14:1.3: “Āvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato sāriputtassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an6.14:2.1: “Tathā tathā, āvuso, bhikkhu vihāraṁ kappeti yathā yathāssa vihāraṁ kappayato na bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, na bhaddikā kālakiriyā.
root_text.an6.14:2.2: Kathañcāvuso, bhikkhu tathā tathā vihāraṁ kappeti yathā yathāssa vihāraṁ kappayato na bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, na bhaddikā kālakiriyā?
root_text.an6.14:3.1: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu kammārāmo hoti kammarato kammārāmataṁ anuyutto, bhassārāmo hoti bhassarato bhassārāmataṁ anuyutto, niddārāmo hoti niddārato niddārāmataṁ anuyutto, saṅgaṇikārāmo hoti saṅgaṇikarato saṅgaṇikārāmataṁ anuyutto, saṁsaggārāmo hoti saṁsaggarato saṁsaggārāmataṁ anuyutto, papañcārāmo hoti papañcarato papañcārāmataṁ anuyutto.
root_text.an6.14:3.2: Evaṁ kho, āvuso, bhikkhu tathā tathā vihāraṁ kappeti yathā yathāssa vihāraṁ kappayato na bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, na bhaddikā kālakiriyā.
root_text.an6.14:3.4: ‘bhikkhu sakkāyābhirato nappajahāsi sakkāyaṁ sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāya’.
root_text.an6.14:4.1: Tathā tathāvuso, bhikkhu vihāraṁ kappeti yathā yathāssa vihāraṁ kappayato bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, bhaddikā kālakiriyā.
root_text.an6.14:4.2: Kathañcāvuso, bhikkhu tathā tathā vihāraṁ kappeti yathā yathāssa vihāraṁ kappayato bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, bhaddikā kālakiriyā?
root_text.an6.14:5.1: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu na kammārāmo hoti na kammarato na kammārāmataṁ anuyutto, na bhassārāmo hoti na bhassarato na bhassārāmataṁ anuyutto, na niddārāmo hoti na niddārato na niddārāmataṁ anuyutto, na saṅgaṇikārāmo hoti na saṅgaṇikarato na saṅgaṇikārāmataṁ anuyutto, na saṁsaggārāmo hoti na saṁsaggarato na saṁsaggārāmataṁ anuyutto, na papañcārāmo hoti na papañcarato na papañcārāmataṁ anuyutto.
root_text.an6.14:5.2: Evaṁ kho, āvuso, bhikkhu tathā tathā vihāraṁ kappeti yathā yathāssa vihāraṁ kappayato bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, bhaddikā kālakiriyā.
root_text.an6.14:5.4: ‘bhikkhu nibbānābhirato pajahāsi sakkāyaṁ sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāyā’ti.

translation_text.an6.14:2.1: “A mendicant lives life so as to not have a good death.
translation_text.an6.14:2.2: And how do they live life so as to not have a good death?
translation_text.an6.14:3.1: Take a mendicant who relishes work, talk, sleep, company, closeness, and proliferation. They love these things and like to relish them.
translation_text.an6.14:3.2: A mendicant who lives life like this does not have a good death.
translation_text.an6.14:3.4: a mendicant who enjoys substantial reality, who hasn’t given up substantial reality to rightly make an end of suffering.
translation_text.an6.14:4.1: A mendicant lives life so as to have a good death.
translation_text.an6.14:4.2: And how do they live life so as to have a good death?
translation_text.an6.14:5.1: Take a mendicant who doesn’t relish work, talk, sleep, company, closeness, and proliferation. They don’t love these things or like to relish them.
translation_text.an6.14:5.2: A mendicant who lives life like this has a good death.
translation_text.an6.14:5.4: a mendicant who delights in extinguishment, who has given up substantial reality to rightly make an end of suffering.
translation_text.an6.14:6.1: A beast who likes to proliferate,
translation_text.an6.14:6.4: the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.an6.14:7.1: But one who gives up proliferation,
translation_text.an6.14:7.4: the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.”

variant_text.an6.14:3.4: nappajahāsi → na pahāsi (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
